novel_features.name: Watch saved searches with diff
novel_features.description: Re-run a stored search and surface what's NEW, REMOVED, or PRICE-CHANGED since the last sync.
novel_features.rationale: apartments.com email alerts only cover new listings — there is no removal alert, no price-drop alert, and no per-listing history. Diffing requires the snapshot history table only this CLI keeps.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent is tracking a relocation over time and needs a reproducible 'what changed since last week' digest, not a fresh search.

novel_features.name: Multi-slug union ranking
novel_features.description: Fan out a search across multiple cities, zips, or neighborhoods and return one ranked, deduped list.
novel_features.rationale: apartments.com URL is single-slug per query — three target neighborhoods means three browser tabs. Fan-out plus listing-URL dedupe lives only in the local store.
novel_features.example: apartments-pp-cli nearby austin-tx round-rock-tx pflugerville-tx --beds 2 --price-max 2500 --rank sqft --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs a single ranked feed across multiple search slugs without writing a fan-out loop.

novel_features.name: Total-cost-of-occupancy ranking
novel_features.description: Rank synced listings by 12-month total cost (rent + pet rent + pet deposit + pet fee), filtered to your hard budget.
novel_features.rationale: apartments.com sort options ignore pet fees entirely. The cost arithmetic happens in local SQL over the synced pet_policy struct.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when budget is binding and pet fees might push a listing over the line.

novel_features.name: $/sqft and $/bed ranking
novel_features.description: Rank synced listings by ratio metrics — price per square foot or price per bedroom.
novel_features.rationale: apartments.com sort omits ratio metrics. Dividing maxrent by sqft or beds in local SQL is one line.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when value-per-dollar is the goal, not 'best match' or 'lowest price'.

novel_features.name: Side-by-side compare
novel_features.description: Pivot 2–8 listings into a wide table — one column per listing — with computed $/sqft and amenity overlap.
novel_features.rationale: apartments.com has no compare view. Local-store pivot lets agents emit a structured wide-table response.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when narrowing a shortlist; the wide table makes amenity-overlap deltas obvious.

novel_features.name: Price-drop alerts
novel_features.description: List listings whose max-rent dropped by ≥N% within a time window.
novel_features.rationale: apartments.com offers no price-drop view — only new-listing emails. Time-window aggregation needs the snapshot history table.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when timing the market or watching for distressed listings.

novel_features.name: Stale-listing flag
novel_features.description: Flag listings whose price and availability haven't changed in N days — often phantom or stuck.
novel_features.rationale: Stuck-listing detection needs the snapshot table — apartments.com has no UI for it.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when a listing seems too good to be true; stale ones often are.

novel_features.name: Phantom-listing detector
novel_features.description: Surface listings flagged by a three-signal join: 404 on re-fetch, dropped from saved-search results, or stale ≥45 days.
novel_features.rationale: No single page exposes 'this listing is probably already leased.' Union of three local-store predicates does.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when prepping a shortlist for tour scheduling — phantoms waste tour slots.

novel_features.name: Neighborhood market summary
novel_features.description: Median, p10, p90 of rent and rent/sqft, pet-friendly share, by city/state and bed count.
novel_features.rationale: apartments.com shows zero distributional stats. Aggregation SQL over the local store does.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs to anchor 'is this a fair price' against the local distribution.

novel_features.name: Listing history
novel_features.description: Time-series of every observation of one listing — rent, availability, status.
novel_features.rationale: Per-listing audit trail comes from the snapshot table only this CLI keeps.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when reasoning about a single listing's price trajectory.

novel_features.name: Weekly digest
novel_features.description: Single-shot composer: new + removed + price-drops + top-5 by $/sqft + stale + phantom flags for one saved search over N days.
novel_features.rationale: Composes the time-series and ranking commands into the actual weekly ritual that exists in user workflows.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs a Monday-morning summary in one call.

novel_features.name: Floor-plan-level value report
novel_features.description: Rank per-floor-plan rent/sqft across synced listings — same building can yield 4 plans at different ratios.
novel_features.rationale: Building-level rent hides plan-level variance. Joining to the floor_plans child table exposes it.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when a building has multiple floor plans and you want the cheap one specifically.

novel_features.name: Amenity must-have intersect
novel_features.description: Filter synced listings to those whose amenities array contains ALL listed terms via FTS5.
novel_features.rationale: apartments.com amenity filter is a fixed curated list — 'natural light' or 'in-unit washer' aren't in it. FTS5 AND-join is.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when the must-haves are free-text, not in apartments.com's amenity dropdown.

novel_features.name: Local shortlist
novel_features.description: Tag-based local shortlist table; add/show/remove listings with notes and tags.
novel_features.rationale: Replaces the user's spreadsheet workflow with a queryable table that joins to listings for compare and digest.
novel_features.example: apartments-pp-cli shortlist add https://www.apartments.com/example-1234 --tag austin --note "liked the kitchen"
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent or user is curating a shortlist; downstream commands like `compare` read from it.

narrative.headline: The apartment-hunt CLI that actually works in 2026 — Surf-cleared bot protection plus a local SQLite store the website itself doesn't have.
narrative.value_prop: Search every Apartments.com listing path-slug from the terminal, sync results to a local SQLite store, and run the workflows the website never built: diff a saved search week-over-week with `watch`, rank by $/sqft net of pet fees with `value`, compare a shortlist side-by-side with `compare`, and surface price drops or phantom listings with `drops`, `stale`, and `phantoms`. Every command is `--json`/`--select`-shaped so an agent can pipe the output without burning context.
narrative.auth_narrative: No authentication required. Apartments.com's anonymous search and listing pages are the entire API surface this CLI uses; saved-search login (cookie session) is intentionally out of scope. Surf with Chrome TLS fingerprint clears the Akamai-style protection at runtime — no clearance cookie capture, no resident browser.

quickstart.comment: First search — verifies Surf transport clears protection and JSON output is well-formed.

quickstart.command: apartments-pp-cli sync-search austin-2br --city austin --state TX --beds 2 --price-max 2500 --pets dog
quickstart.comment: Persist that search to the local store under the slug 'austin-2br' so transcendence commands can read it.
Rank the synced listings by $/sqft — the ratio metric apartments.com's sort omits.
After a few days, run this — `watch` diffs against the previous sync and emits NEW / REMOVED / PRICE-CHANGED sets.
The Monday-morning digest: new + removed + price drops + top-by-$/sqft + stale + phantoms in one structured output.

troubleshoots.fix: Run `apartments-pp-cli doctor` to verify Surf transport is active. If 403 persists, the Chrome TLS fingerprint may need refresh — file an issue with the doctor output.

troubleshoots.symptom: watch reports no changes when you know listings changed
troubleshoots.fix: Confirm a previous `sync` exists: `apartments-pp-cli sql "SELECT MAX(observed_at) FROM listing_snapshots WHERE saved_search = 'austin-2br'"`. If empty, run sync first.

troubleshoots.symptom: value or rank command returns empty
troubleshoots.fix: These read from the local store. Run `sync` first. Check with `apartments-pp-cli sql "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listings"`.

troubleshoots.symptom: amenity must-have intersect returns no rows
troubleshoots.fix: FTS5 needs amenities populated; some listings have empty amenity arrays. Try `apartments-pp-cli sql "SELECT url, length(amenities) FROM listings ORDER BY length(amenities) DESC LIMIT 5"` to confirm.

troubleshoots.symptom: rate limited or repeated 429s during sync
troubleshoots.fix: Sync uses adaptive pacing automatically. If you hit a wall, pause for 30 seconds and re-run; cliutil.AdaptiveLimiter will back off.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent or human needs structured Apartments.com data and needs the workflows the website itself doesn't expose: cross-search diffs over time, $/sqft and total-cost-of-occupancy rankings, multi-slug union queries, side-by-side comparison, and digest-style summaries. Reach for it for relocation tracking, value-per-dollar screens across shortlists, leasing-agent weekly digests, and any rental-search scenario that needs JSON + offline composition. Skip it for one-off browsing — the apartments.com website is fine for that.

recipes.title: Three-neighborhood relocation hunt
recipes.command: apartments-pp-cli nearby austin-tx round-rock-tx pflugerville-tx --beds 2 --price-max 2500 --pets dog --rank sqft --json --select url,addressLocality,maxrent,sqft,price_per_sqft
recipes.explanation: Single ranked feed across three target neighborhoods, deduped by listing URL, with `--select` keeping only the columns the agent needs.

recipes.title: Weekly leasing-agent digest
recipes.explanation: Composes new / removed / price-drops / top-5-by-sqft / stale / phantom flags for a single client search into a markdown report ready to paste into email.

recipes.title: Total-cost screen with hard budget
recipes.explanation: Ranks the synced listings by 12-month total cost (rent + pet rent + pet deposit + pet fee), filtered to under-budget rows only.

recipes.title: Phantom-detection sweep before tour scheduling
recipes.explanation: Three-signal union — 404, dropped from saved search, stale ≥45 days. Filter your shortlist before booking tours so you don't drive across town to a leased unit.

recipes.title: Offline FTS amenity intersect
recipes.explanation: Path filter for amenities apartments.com's filter dropdown doesn't expose. FTS5 AND-join over the synced amenities array.

trigger_phrases: rank rentals by price per square foot
